name: precedence: Eric (cleaner AND active guest) replying '2' in a numbered guest menu drives his booking, not a room clean (B11.5)
comment: Eric's number (27825999279) is both a registered cleaner and an active guest with a pending numbered menu (CONFIRMED expects 1=gate / 2=cancel). '2' is also Room 02's number. Before B11.5 the cleaner-naming global '*' transition preempted routing and marked Room 02 clean. Now the guest-side expectation wins: the booking is cancelled and Room 02 is untouched (exactly two writes, neither on WS_Rooms). This is the ambiguous case — both a pending guest menu AND a matching cleaning room — decided in favour of the guest state machine. (b11 has no hourly flow; CONFIRMED's numbered menu is the numbered guest state that exists here — the fix is read from states.json and generalises to the hourly duration / occupancy menus once B9/F19 merge.) Mutation target #1.
